top_signals.title: UN estimates 80 mines remain in Hormuz
top_signals.summary: The United Nations’ International Maritime Organization said Friday that approximately 80 mines remain in historic shipping lanes in the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting the challenge of restoring normal operations in the critical oil transit route. The Iran war created two alternative shipping channels through Hormuz. One route follows Oman’s coast and is coordinated by the ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Surge in Asia-US container rates continues, tanker rates fall as Strait of Hormuz transits rise
top_signals.summary: Rates for shipping containers from east Asia and China to the US continued to surge as importers pull volumes forward ahead of possible new tariffs, while liquid tanker rates continued to soften amid a tentative ceasefire in the Middle East. STRAIT OF HORMUZ UPDATE The flow of vessels through the Strait of Hormuz increased this ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Tanker Market Now a “Hostage” of Geopolitics
top_signals.summary: The tanker market has felt the full force of geopolitical factors having an impact on freight rates, demand and cargo flows/ton-mile demand, since the start of 2025. In its latest weekly report, shipbroker Gibson said that “after a chaotic 2025 dominated by tariffs, trade wars, actual wars and sanctions, it seemed hard to imagine 2026 ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Wärtsilä warns of hurdles for low-carbon bunker use amid IMO delays
top_signals.summary: Shipping companies are facing greater challenges in transitioning to low-carbon fuels amid an uncertain, fragmented regulatory outlook following the International Maritime Organization’s failure to adopt a global decarbonization framework, according to the top marine executive of Finnish technology group Wärtsilä. The UN agency in October 2025 voted to delay the discussions of the adoption of ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Scorpio Tankers resumes Persian Gulf transit; LR2 to deliver jet fuel to Europe for $10 million
top_signals.summary: Scorpio Tankers, the world’s largest LR2 shipping company by fleet size, has commenced transiting through the Strait of Hormuz, with one of its LR2s chartered by Admic for $10 million to deliver jet fuel in Europe, three sources with tanker brokerages and charterers said June 25. This marked the first direct clean tanker spot fixture ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Brexit Makes Inflation Spirals More Likely, Says Bank of England Economist Pill - Bloomberg.com
top_signals.summary: Brexit Makes Inflation Spirals More Likely, Says Bank of England Economist Pill  Bloomberg.com
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Burnham’s choice of Chancellor will define his premiership
top_signals.summary: Economic policy is so fraught that Burnham must choose from various paths. Retaining Reeves means warmed-over Starmerism; Miliband would be a sign that the Labour Party is tacking left; and choosing Streeting would be an advertisement that the Blairites were getting the band back together. What signal will the next Prime Minister seek to send? [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: War, Weather & Tragedy: Why Coal Demand Could Surge in 2026
top_signals.summary: Global coal demand has been rising over the last couple of years, with new record highs reached in both 2024 and 2025 at around 8.8 billion tonnes, according to the International Energy Agency (IEA). However, growth has slowed and speculation regarding “peak coal” has emerged as the world continues to decarbonize. Advanced economies are phasing ...

top_signals.title: Protein coffee? Brands cash in on functional beverage boom
top_signals.summary: "We're selling [almost] as much protein cold foam as we do flat whites," Starbuck's EMEA Manager of Beverage Development Sam Henderson told CNBC.

top_signals.title: VLCCs Down Last Week
top_signals.summary: Clean LR2 The TC1 75kt MEG/Japan index climbed 18.88 points this week to WS509.44. A voyage west also freighted up this week with the TC20 90kt MEG/UK-Continent index going from $9.38 million to $9.93 million. The TC15 80kt Mediterranean/East index dropped a modest $37,000 to $4.32 million mark this week, with the corresponding TCE at ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Dry Bulk Market: Capesizes Endured a Difficult Week
top_signals.summary: Capesize The market endured a difficult week, with early cautious optimism quickly giving way to broader weakness across both basins. The week opened on a relatively balanced footing following the previous Friday’s stronger finish, supported by firmer South Brazil and West Africa activity in the Atlantic and steady miner participation in the Pacific.
